product_name: SpatialFlow Agent, built on a Codex-derived foundation
goal: Input one open-source empty-room image and transform it into a Nordic-style living room while preserving the floor, windows, walls, camera viewpoint, and room structure. Use the latest verified stack: SAM 3/SAM 3.1 + DINO-X for perception, Depth Anything 3 + Depth Pro + UniDepthV2 for depth/layout, OpenRouter openai/gpt-5.4 for planning, FLUX.2 [dev] for editing, and Qwen3-VL + SigLIP 2 for verification.

model_stack.purpose: Detect and segment walls, floor, ceiling, windows, doors, furniture, decor, fixed structure, and editable regions.

fallback: Depth Anything V2 Large smoke-test fallback
RANSAC / vanishing-line / rule-based room envelope fitting
model_stack.purpose: Estimate metric depth, planes, room envelope, free space, scale constraints, and geometry hints for editing.
Turn user intent and spatial state into an ordered edit plan and verifier-aware action sequence.

fallback: FLUX.1 Kontext [dev] / FLUX.1 Fill only as fallback
model_stack.purpose: Perform local/global image editing, inpainting, furniture insertion, restyling, relighting, and repair.

fallback: Geometry rules over masks, boxes, depth, and planes
model_stack.purpose: Score structure preservation, style alignment, object plausibility, collisions, window/wall blocking, floating furniture, and artifact quality.

tools.name: room_spatial_parser
tools.description: Use SAM 3/SAM 3.1 where available, DINO-X + SAM, and Qwen3-VL to detect/segment walls, floor, windows, doors, furniture, and fixed structure.

tools.name: depth_layout_estimator
tools.description: Use Depth Anything 3 / DA3METRIC-LARGE where available, Apple Depth Pro, UniDepthV2, and geometric fitting to estimate metric depth, planes, room envelope, and editable free space.

tools.name: layout_action_planner
tools.description: Use openrouter openai/gpt-5.4 to plan room-design edit actions over spatial state, hard constraints, and business intent.

tools.name: visual_edit_executor
tools.description: Use FLUX.2 [dev] as primary local B200 editing stack; fallback to Qwen-Image-Edit or FLUX.1 Kontext/Fill when needed.

tools.name: visual_verifier
tools.description: Use Qwen3.5, SigLIP 2 giant/SO400M, and geometry rules to check style match, structure preservation, non-intersection, no wall/window blocking, and artifact quality. Qwen3-VL is fallback only.

criteria: furniture layout is plausible and not floating/intersecting
output is useful for a realtor/designer workflow
